# BudgetIQ 💰

A personal finance tracker built with React, Vite, and Supabase. Track income and expenses, set budgets, manage savings goals, and get real-time insights — all in a clean dark dashboard.

![BudgetIQ Dashboard](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Features

- **Dashboard** — live balance, income, expenses, and savings rate with this-month filtering
- **Transactions** — add, edit, delete, and search transactions with month/category filters and CSV export
- **Recurring transactions** — mark transactions as weekly or monthly recurring with auto-scheduling
- **Budgets** — set monthly spending limits per category with real-time progress and over-budget alerts
- **Goals** — create savings goals and deposit incrementally until complete
- **Analytics** — expense and income breakdowns, insights, and income vs expenses ratio
- **Multi-currency** — live exchange rates via open.er-api.com, supports USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, NGN
- **Auth** — email/password login and registration via Supabase Auth with protected routes
- **Toast notifications** — success, error, info, and warning feedback throughout the app
- **Settings** — update display name, currency, savings rate target, and danger zone data wipe

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Tech |
|---|---|
| Frontend | React 18, Vite |
| Backend / DB | Supabase (PostgreSQL) |
| Auth | Supabase Auth |
| Routing | React Router DOM v6 |
| Charts | Recharts |
| Currency | open.er-api.com |
| Deployment | Vercel |

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+
- A free [Supabase](https://supabase.com) account

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/danielex223/budgetiq.git
cd budgetiq
```

### 2. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

### 3. Set up Supabase

Create a new project on [supabase.com](https://supabase.com), then run the following in the SQL Editor:

```sql
create table transactions (
  id bigint generated by default as identity primary key,
  user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  type text not null check (type in ('income', 'expense')),
  category text not null,
  amount numeric not null,
  note text default '',
  recurring boolean default false,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

create table budgets (
  id bigint generated by default as identity primary key,
  user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  category text not null,
  "limit" numeric not null,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

create table goals (
  id bigint generated by default as identity primary key,
  user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  name text not null,
  target numeric not null,
  saved numeric default 0,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

alter table transactions enable row level security;
alter table budgets enable row level security;
alter table goals enable row level security;

create policy "users own transactions" on transactions for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);
create policy "users own budgets" on budgets for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);
create policy "users own goals" on goals for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

### 4. Run the app

```bash
npm run dev
```

Visit `http://localhost:5173`, register an account, and start tracking.

---

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── lib/
│   ├── supabase.js              # Supabase client
│   ├── currencyUtils.js         # Currency conversion + formatting
│   ├── recurringTransactions.js # Recurring transaction logic
│   └── useToast.js              # Toast notification context
├── components/
│   ├── Navbar.jsx               # Sidebar navigation
│   ├── ProtectedRoute.jsx       # Auth guard
│   └── Toast.jsx                # Toast UI + container
├── pages/
│   ├── Login.jsx
│   ├── Register.jsx
│   ├── Landing.jsx
│   ├── Dashboard.jsx
│   ├── Transactions.jsx
│   ├── Budgets.jsx
│   ├── Goals.jsx
│   ├── Analytics.jsx
│   └── Settings.jsx
└── App.jsx
```

---

## Deployment

This app is deployed on budgetiq-75rz19kwe-danielex223s-projects.vercel.app . To deploy your own:

1. Push your repo to GitHub
2. Import the project on vercel.com
3. Deploy
